text:
The interview used heavily throughout this video where the workers get blown features an outlandishly fake title and marquee design, something that was apparently in the same vein of humor we YTP junkies love. John Morgan of "Zoo Keeper" fame reminisced back in 2001:
"In 1982, Keith Egging was the Director of Creativity (or some weird title like that). He always had a human skull on his desk which opened up on a hinge and was filled with Hershey's kisses. One day a TV news crew came over to do some interview, and Keith showed them around. He took them back into our area and really played it up (fed them some pretty thick bull which they really ate it up). He took them to the farthest office of cubicles and said that this was DEEP THOUGHT - where all the heaviest thinking took place. Of course this was just made up (the office being picked for it's distance only), but hey, they bought it. The main programmers (me, Mark Blazczyk and Rex Battenberg) took off on this and other of Keith's tall tales and frequently made up whoppers about everything under the sun to one up each other with our far fetched stories. Pretty cool for those days."

Mark Blazczyk was able to comment as well:
"It is correct that Toasters & Chainsaws was never a real game. It certainly was never tested. All it was was a marquee. Keith Egging came up with the name to try to trigger our creativity to come up with some new game concepts."

All of this is the result of me trying to find out if Toasters and Chainsaws was one of YOUR jokes, or actually part of the video. Turns out the joke hole went pretty deep.
